title: Mob Race
description: Be the first to kill either all unique mobs or simply the majority. Play in either teams or in one big free for all.
body:
This datapack has several settings which change how the game is played. Below is a list of the different game modes and how they work.


# Gamemodes

Regular Free for All: In a regular free for all you play against a number of opponents as you are all trying to be the first to kill all 80 (81 for 1.21.6+) unique mobs in the game.

Lockout Free for All: When playing a lockout only the first player to kill a speciic mob will recevie a point for it, that means the winner isn't the first to kill all 80 mobs, but the first to kill so many that no other player can surpass them (this also makes it possible to have a draw between multiple players)

##

Regular Team Match: Just like in a Regular Free for All the goal here is to kill all 80 unique mobs first, but now you must sort yourself into two teams (blue and red), and work together to do so faster than the opoosing team.

Lockout Team Match: In a lockout team match it, just like in a lockout free for all, isn't possible for both teams to receive a point for the same mob type. This effectively means that it becomes a race to 41 points, as there cannot be more than 2 teams.



# (/Trigger) Commands

## /Trigger Team_Mode  -  /Trigger Team_Mode set 0
_Enables and disables team mode respectively_

### /Trigger Join_Blue_Team  -  /Trigger Join_Red_Team
(Can only be used if team mode is enabled) _Lets you join the team the command is referencing. If you join a team while already in one you will automatically leaves the old one as well._


## /Trigger Lockout  -  /Trigger Lockout set 0
_Enables and disables Lockout mode respectively_


## /Trigger Start
_Starts the match, removing the spawn box which prevents players from simply running away before the match has started_



## /Trigger Location
(Can only be used in team mode after the match has started) _Will send the coordinates of all your teammates' in the chat (It will only be visible to yourself)_


## /Trigger Remaining_Mobs
(Can only be used after the match has started) _Will display a message in the chat which contains all mobs that will still grant you points (one mob per line in the chat)_



# Notes
- The datapack has a 'settings' folder, through which you can disable command explanations as well as PvP among teammates
- When playing in Lockout mode the game automatically makes use of what is commonly referred to as a 'mercy rule', which means it will end the game once the first place spot is guaranteed
- All commands that are available before the match starts are unavailable after it does, and vice versa
- Players can join after the match is started, if it is a free for all match they can participate directly, while if it is a team match, they will be able to join a team.




Please do contact me on discord with any issues.
